UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user registered on the SwapIt platform, managing their personal information, listed items, and swap history.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

username.description: A unique username chosen by the user for identification on the platform.

email.description: The user's unique email address, used for communication and account recovery.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

bio.description: A short biographical description provided by the user, visible on their public profile.

location.description: The user's geographical location (e.g., city, state, country) for local swap matching.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

registrationDate.description: The timestamp when the user registered on the SwapIt platform.

lastLoginDate.description: The timestamp of the user's most recent login.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines categories that items can belong to, facilitating browsing and search.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the item category (e.g., 'Electronics', 'Books', 'Clothing').

description.description: A brief explanation or characteristics of the category.

Item.title: Item
Item.description: Represents an item that a user has listed for swapping on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Item entity.

title.description: The short, descriptive title of the item.

description.description: A detailed description of the item, potentially AI-generated or refined.

condition.description: The physical condition of the item.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this item belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Item)

imageUrls.description: An array of URLs pointing to images of the item for visual representation.

ownerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns and listed this item. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Item)

listedDate.description: The timestamp when the item was first listed on the platform.

status.description: The current availability status of the item (e.g., Available, Pending Swap, Swapped, Removed).

preferredSwapItemsDescription.description: A textual description of the types of items the owner is looking for in exchange.

SwapProposal.title: SwapProposal
SwapProposal.description: Represents a proposal made by one user to exchange items with another user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SwapProposal entity.

proposerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who initiated this swap proposal. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SwapProposal)

targetItemId.description: Reference to the Item that the proposer wishes to acquire. (Relationship: Item 1:N SwapProposal)

offeredItemIds.description: An array of references to the Items that the proposer is offering in exchange. (Relationship: Item N:N SwapProposal - indirectly, via an array of IDs)

proposalDate.description: The timestamp when the swap proposal was created.

status.description: The current status of the swap proposal (e.g., Pending, Accepted, Declined, Cancelled, Completed).

message.description: An optional message included by the proposer with their offer.

responseDate.description: The timestamp when the proposal received a response (accepted/declined).

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the authenticated user whose `uid` matches `{userId}`. The `id` field within the document must match `{userId}`. This path ensures path-based ownership for private user data.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Contains globally accessible and typically read-only categories for items. All users can read documents in this collection.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the category.

definition.description: Stores items listed by users. This nested path ensures strong ownership, where `{ownerId}` matches `request.auth.uid`. The `ownerId` field is also redundantly stored within the document. Items with `status: 'Available'` are readable by all users via collection group queries on 'items', supporting global browsing, while write access is restricted to the item's owner.

params.name: ownerId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns and listed the item.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique ID of the item.

definition.description: Manages all swap proposals. Documents include `proposerId` and a **denormalized `targetItemOwnerId`** for authorization independence. Access is granted to either the `proposerId` or the `targetItemOwnerId`, enabling efficient incoming/outgoing proposal queries without `get()` calls in rules. This crucial denormalization ensures QAP compliance and simplifies security rules for this two-party interaction.

params.name: swapProposalId
params.description: The unique ID of the swap proposal.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for SwapIt prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable access patterns by adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This structure explicitly avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies that would require `get()` calls in security rules. For example:
*   `UserProfile` documents are stored at `/users/{userId}`, where `userId` matches `request.auth.uid`, making authorization direct and explicit.
*   `Item` documents are stored under `/users/{ownerId}/items/{itemId}`. The `ownerId` in the path directly ties ownership to the authenticated user, eliminating the need to `get()` a parent user document to check permissions. The `ownerId` is also redundantly stored within the `Item` document to allow for flexible querying and further reinforce security.
*   `SwapProposal` documents are stored in a top-level collection `/swapProposals/{swapProposalId}`. To ensure authorization independence for both the proposer and the target item owner, the `ownerId` of the `targetItemId` is **denormalized** into the `SwapProposal` document as `targetItemOwnerId`. This crucial denormalization allows rules to check `request.auth.uid` against `proposerId` or `targetItemOwnerId` directly within the `SwapProposal` document itself, without needing to `get()` the `Item` document or its owner.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters) and Structural Segregation:** The design facilitates Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) by ensuring that security rules do not act as filters on large datasets, and by segregating data based on its security posture:
*   **Private User Data:** `UserProfile` data is stored under `/users/{userId}`, ensuring that users can only access their own profiles. Listing a user's own profile is a direct `get()` or a collection query on a very small scope (their own subcollection).
*   **User-Owned Items:** Items are stored under `/users/{ownerId}/items/{itemId}`. A user can easily list all their own items via `db.collection('users').doc(request.auth.uid).collection('items')`. For global browsing of available items (as required by "Browse and Search Items"), a collection group query on `items` subcollections can be performed, with rules ensuring that only items with `status: 'Available'` are readable by unauthenticated or other authenticated users. This combination enables both direct owner access and global discovery in a QAP-compliant manner, by offloading the 'public' filtering to the `status` field in rules.
*   **Public Static Data:** `Category` documents are stored in a top-level `/categories` collection, making them globally readable by all users, which is ideal for dropdowns and filtering UIs.
*   **Private Interaction Data:** `SwapProposal` documents are in a top-level `/swapProposals`. Users can query for "my incoming proposals" by filtering `where('targetItemOwnerId', '==', request.auth.uid)` and "my outgoing proposals" by filtering `where('proposerId', '==', request.auth.uid)`. These queries are efficient and directly align with QAPs, as `proposerId` and `targetItemOwnerId` are explicitly denormalized for direct access by rules.

**Consistency and Predictability:** Semantic naming conventions are used (e.g., `userId`, `ownerId`, `swapProposalId`), and explicit `status` fields are used in `Item` and `SwapProposal` entities to clearly define state transitions, aiding in debugging and rule clarity. The structural hierarchy for user-owned items reinforces ownership.
